name: Residential Capacity Expansion
consequences: Physical capacity exists for 12 suites, but expansion is deferred until the pilot proves acceptable data quality, manageable false alarm rates, safe staffing ratios, and usable annotation throughput.
options: Expand to Full Capacity (12 Suites)
Maintain Initial Scale with 8 Suites
Invest in Suite Upgrades before Expansion
review: Deferred capacity expansion risks missing a crucial window for cost-effective growth as pilot data quality improves.

name: Event-Triage Algorithm Development
consequences: The scientific program has three explicit aims: Aim 3, develop and benchmark semi-automated event-triage tools that rank probable parasomnia episodes...
options: Invest in Rule-Based Systems for Event Triage
Implement Machine Learning Approaches with Human Oversight
Adopt a Hybrid Model Combining Both Rule-Based and ML Components
review: Leveraging rule-based systems may overlook complex edge cases, while machine learning approaches risk introducing unforeseen biases.

name: Sensor Acquisition Model Optimization
consequences: Sensing uses a tiered acquisition model: nightly low-burden monitoring via dry-electrode EEG headbands...
options: Increase Use of Contact-Free Mattress Sensors and Environmental Sensors
Reduce Enhanced Nights with Fuller PSG Montage and Focus on Dry-Electrode Monitoring
Invest in Higher-Fidelity but More Expensive Sensing Technology
review: Overreliance on dry-electrode monitoring may compromise physiological resolution during critical events.

name: Participant Recruitment Strategies
consequences: Participant recruitment targets adults aged 18–65 with NREM parasomnia confirmed through a structured pre-admission pathway...
options: Increase Referrals from Specialist Clinics and Sleep Centers
Expand to Online Platforms for Participant Recruitment and Informed Consent
Enhance the Pre-Admission Pathway with Additional Diagnostic Tools
review: Relying solely on specialist referrals may overlook potential participants in non-specialized settings.

name: Budget Allocation Strategies
consequences: The budget is €3.8 million over 3 years, gated: €1.8M year one...
options: Prioritize Staffing and Personnel Costs in Year One
Allocate More Funds to Participant Compensation and Data Storage in Year Two
Delay Event-Triage Algorithm Development until Year Three
review: Underfunding data storage and retention may hinder long-term dataset viability.

name: Governance Structure and Scientific Advisory Board
consequences: Governance is a 3-member external scientific advisory board reviewing progress every 6 months...
options: Increase Frequency of Progress Reviews to Quarterly Meetings
Expand the External Scientific Advisory Board with Additional Members from Diverse Disciplines
Establish an Internal Peer Review Process for Grant Funding Proposals
review: A lack of diverse perspectives on the advisory board may lead to biases in decision-making.

name: Pharmaceutical Industry Partnership Opportunities
consequences: Funding sources are DFG research grant and University of Bonn internal research funding as the primary pillars...
options: Seek a Pharmaceutical Partner for Optional Funding and Co-Publication Rights
Explore Government Agency Partnerships for Additional Funding Opportunities
Develop a Model for Public-Private Partnership in Sleep Research
review: Overemphasis on pharmaceutical partnerships may compromise academic integrity.

name: Physiological Signal Processing Upgrades
consequences: Upgrading signal processing pipelines will improve data quality and reduce manual review burden, but may introduce novel artifacts or biases in the process.
options: Deep-learning-based artifact removal to minimize false positives
Adaptive filtering techniques to preserve physiological resolution during enhanced nights
Graph-theoretic analysis of networked EEG data to capture complex relationships
review: Signal processing upgrades may inadvertently create new artifacts or bias the data, which can undermine the validity of downstream analyses.

name: Participant Retention Strategies
consequences: Implementing participant retention strategies will improve compliance and reduce dropout rates, but may compromise data quality if participants become too comfortable.
options: Gamified sleep diaries to encourage active engagement
Incentivized goal-setting and progress tracking for improved motivation
Community-building activities to foster a sense of belonging among participants
review: Participant retention strategies may inadvertently create artificial social pressures or expectations, which can influence data quality and skew results.

name: Research Data Storage and Backup
consequences: Enhancing research data storage and backup will ensure long-term preservation of sensitive information, but may increase operational costs and complexity.
options: Cloud-based data archiving for disaster recovery and redundancy
Distributed, decentralized data storage to prioritize participant privacy
Hybrid models combining local NAS with cloud backup for optimal trade-offs
review: Over-reliance on cloud storage may create single points of failure or compromise participant confidentiality, especially in the context of sensitive neurological data.

name: Event-Triage Tool Development
consequences: Developing event-triage tools will improve efficiency and reduce manual review burden, but may introduce errors or misclassify events.
options: Machine-learning-based approaches to differentiate parasomnia from normal behavior
Rule-based systems leveraging expert-derived criteria for accurate classification
Hybrid models combining machine learning with rule-based approaches for optimal trade-offs
review: Event-triage tools may inadvertently prioritize speed over accuracy, which can lead to misclassifications and compromise the validity of downstream analyses.

name: Participant Engagement and Education
consequences: Enhancing participant engagement and education will improve compliance and reduce dropout rates, but may compromise data quality if participants become too comfortable.
options: Interactive educational modules on sleep disorders and research procedures
Personalized goal-setting and progress tracking for improved motivation
Community-building activities to foster a sense of belonging among participants
review: Participant engagement strategies may inadvertently create artificial social pressures or expectations, which can influence data quality and skew results.

name: Governance Structure Review
consequences: Reviewing the governance structure will ensure accountability and transparency, but may compromise operational flexibility and speed.
options: Establishing a formal data safety board to oversee participant confidentiality
Implementing a hierarchical decision-making process for budget reallocations
Developing a centralized database management system for optimal data control
review: Governance structure reviews may inadvertently create overly bureaucratic or rigid operational frameworks, which can hinder project progress and efficiency.

name: Scientific Advisory Board Reconfiguration
consequences: Reconfiguring the scientific advisory board will ensure diverse perspectives and expertise, but may compromise project momentum and speed.
options: Diversifying the board composition to include domain experts from various fields
Establishing a rotating membership process for fresh insights and ideas
Implementing a formal feedback mechanism for continuous improvement
review: Scientific advisory board reconfigurations may inadvertently create conflicts or inconsistencies among board members, which can compromise project consensus and progress.
